title: microstegium spectabile
intro: Microstegium spectabile, also known as Finger Grass, is a non-native, invasive grass species native to Asia and the Pacific Islands, commonly found in North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to its rich content of saponins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Finger Grass include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. Finger Grass can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a medical professional due to its invasive nature and potential side effects.
